items.title: File upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation. This was part of a broader set of Adobe security updates released on this date.

items.title: Improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This vulnerability was addressed in Adobe's July 2026 security updates.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This regularly scheduled security update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass. Adobe has also released an isolated patch for these issues.

items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, 1 critical
items.summary: Amasty issued a significant security release for 25 of its Magento extensions, including one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes (CVE-2026-53787) allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in. This vulnerability opens the door to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento (PolyShell)
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, rated 9.8 (critical). A crafted cookie on any storefront page can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.
